methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $125M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
NODA AI, a Series A defense and security company founded by Philong Duong and Dave Corbett, was most recently valued at $125M post-money on $29M raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public companies in the Defense & Security sector at the same or adjacent stage, including peers such as Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 2.0x multiplier to the $125M last post-money valuation, implying a next-stage value of $250M. Peer-median analysis examines seven same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables. The median valuation across this set is $1.0B, with a range from $300M (floor) to $3.0B (ceiling). Named peers in this bucket include Dream Security at $3.0B (Series B), Harmattan AI at $1.4B (Series B), Code Metal at $1.2B (Series B), and Castelion and Defense Unicorns each at $1.0B (Series B).

Triangulation across both methods yields a valuation range of $212.5M (low), $625M (median), and $1.15B (high). The $212.5M floor reflects conservative application of stage-progression methodology alone. The median of $625M represents equal weighting across both approaches. The primary uncertainty lies in peer comparability: the comp set spans a wide valuation band and is dominated by Series B finalists, creating ambiguity around stage-appropriate placement for an early Series A company.
